metadata.summary: Client library for accessing an IQM quantum computer

documentation:
---
substitutions:
  Black badge: |-
    ```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg
    :target: https://github.com/psf/black
    ```
  CI badge: |-

{{ CI badge }} {{ Release badge }} {{ Black badge }}

# IQM Client

Client-side Python library for connecting to an [IQM](https://meetiqm.com/) quantum computer.

## Installation

IQM client is not intended to be used directly by human users. For executing code on an IQM quantum computer,
you can use for example the [Qiskit on IQM](https://iqm-finland.github.io/qiskit-on-iqm/) library.

If you want just this library, though, you can install it from the Python Package Index (PyPI):

```bash
$ pip install iqm-client
```

## Documentation

Documentation for the latest version is [available online](https://iqm-finland.github.io/iqm-client/).
You can build documentation for any older version locally by cloning the Git repository, checking out the
corresponding tag, and running the docs builder. For example, to build the documentation for version `15.4`:

```bash
$ git clone git@github.com:iqm-finland/iqm-client.git
$ cd iqm-client
$ git checkout 15.4
$ tox run -e docs
```

(Unless you need to build documentation for multiple versions, consider passing `git clone` options to
only clone the commit for the specific version tag, e.g. `--branch 15.4 --depth 1` for version `15.4`.
This will be much faster than cloning the full repository, because some large files have been stored
in the commit history.)

`tox run -e docs` will build the documentation at `./build/sphinx/html`. This command requires the `tox,`, `sphinx` and
`sphinx-book-theme` Python packages (see the `docs` optional dependency in `pyproject.toml`);
you can install the necessary packages with `pip install -e ".[dev,docs]"`

## Copyright

IQM client is free software, released under the Apache License, version 2.0.

Copyright 2021-2024 IQM client developers.

documentation: Supported API endpoints.

documentation: IQM Resonance and Cocos-based circuits execution

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.api.APIVariant.V2

Supported API versions and variants.

WARNING:
Only .V1 is considered stable. The .V2 API is experimental and
is subject to change without notice, even in minor or patch releases.
Use with caution, as future updates may introduce breaking changes or remove functionality entirely.

documentation:
Args:
    variant: API variant.
    iqm_server_url: URL of the IQM server,
                    e.g. https://test.qc.iqm.fi/cocos or https://cocos.resonance.meetiqm.com/garnet for .V1
                    or https://test.qc.iqm.fi for .V2

documentation:
Returns:
    Relative URLs for each supported API endpoints.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.api.APIConfig.is_supported
documentation:
Args:
    endpoint: API endpoint.

Returns:

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.api.APIConfig.url
documentation:
Args:
    endpoint: API endpoint.
    args: Arguments to be passed to the URL.

Returns:
    URL for the given endpoint.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the endpoint is not supported.
Provides supported API endpoints for a given API variant.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.authentication.TokenManager

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.authentication.TokenManager.time_left_seconds
documentation:
Check how much time is left until the token expires.

Returns:
    Time left on token in seconds.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.authentication.TokenManager.get_bearer_token
documentation:
Returns a valid bearer token, or None if no user authentication has been configured.

Raises:
    ClientAuthenticationError: getting the token failed

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.authentication.TokenManager.close
documentation:
Close the configured token provider.

Returns:
    True if closing was successful

Raises:
    ClientAuthenticationError: closing failed

TokenManager manages the access token required for user authentication.

Args:
    token: Long-lived IQM token in plain text format
    tokens_file: Path to a tokens file used for authentication
    auth_server_url: Base URL of the authentication server
    username: Username to log in to authentication server
    password: Password to log in to authentication server

The parameters can also be read from the environment variables IQM_TOKEN, IQM_TOKENS_FILE,
IQM_AUTH_SERVER, IQM_AUTH_USERNAME, IQM_AUTH_PASSWORD. Environment variables can not be
mixed with initialisation arguments. All parameters must come from the same source.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.authentication.TokenProviderInterface

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.authentication.TokenProviderInterface.get_token
documentation:
Returns a valid access token.

Raises:
    ClientAuthenticationError: acquiring the token failed

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.authentication.TokenProviderInterface.close
documentation:
Closes authentication session.

Raises:
    ClientAuthenticationError: closing the session failed
Interface to token provider

functions.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.transpile_insert_moves
documentation:
Inserts MOVEs to the circuit according to a given architecture specification.

The function does nothing if the given architecture specification does not support MOVE gates.
Note that this method assumes that the circuit is already transpiled to a coupling map/architecture where the
resonator has been abstracted away, i.e. the edges of the coupling map that contain resonators are replaced by
edges between the other qubit and all qubits that can be moved to that resonator.

Args:

: circuit: The circuit to add MOVE instructions to.
  arch: Restrictions of the target device
  existing_moves: Specifies how to deal with existing MOVE instruction,

  > If None, the function will use ExistingMoveHandlingOptions.REMOVE with a user warning if there are move
  > instructions in the circuit.

  qubit_mapping: Mapping of logical qubit names to physical qubit names.

  : Can be set to `None` if all `circuits` already use physical qubit names.

functions.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile._transpile_insert_moves
documentation:
Inserts MOVE gates into a list of instructions and changes the existing instructions as needed.

Helper function for {func}`transpile_insert_moves`.

Args:

: instructions: The instructions in the circuit.
  res_status: The location of the resonator states at the start of the instructions. At
  the end of this method this tracker is adjusted to reflect the state at the end of the returned instructions.
  arch: The target quantum architecture.
  qubit_mapping: Mapping from logical qubit names to physical qubit names.

Raises:
: CircuitTranspilationError: Raised when the circuit contains invalid gates that cannot be transpiled using this

  : method.

Returns:

: The transpiled list of instructions.

functions.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.transpile_remove_moves
documentation:
Removes MOVE gates from a circuit.

The method assumes that these MOVE gates are moving the resonator state in and out the resonator register to
reconstruct the CZ gates. If this is not the case, the semantic equivalence cannot be guaranteed.

Args:
    circuit: The circuit from which the MOVE gates need to be removed.

Returns:
    The circuit with the MOVE gates removed and the targets for all other gates updated accordingly.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ExistingMoveHandlingOptions

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ExistingMoveHandlingOptions.KEEP
documentation: Transpiler will keep the MOVE instructions as specified checking if they are correct, adding more as needed.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ExistingMoveHandlingOptions.REMOVE
documentation: Transpiler will remove the instructions and add new ones.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ExistingMoveHandlingOptions.TRUST
documentation: Transpiler will keep the MOVE instructions without checking if they are correct, and add more as needed.
Transpile options for handling of existing MOVE instructions.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker.from_dynamic_architecture
documentation:
Constructor to make the ResonatorStateTracker from a DynamicQuantumArchitecture.

Args:
    arch: The architecture to track the resonator state on.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker.from_circuit
documentation:
Constructor to make the ResonatorStateTracker from a circuit.

Infers the resonator connectivity from the MOVE gates in the circuit.

Args:
    circuit: The circuit to track the resonator state on.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker.from_instructions
documentation:
Constructor to make the ResonatorStateTracker from a sequence of instructions.

Infers the resonator connectivity from the MOVE gates.

Args:
    instructions: The instructions to track the resonator state on.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker.resonators
documentation: Getter for the resonator registers that are being tracked.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker.supports_move
documentation: Bool whether any MOVE gate is allowed.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker.apply_move
documentation:
Apply the logical changes of the resonator state location when a MOVE gate between qubit and resonator is
applied.

Args:
    qubit: The moved qubit.
    resonator: The moved resonator.

Raises:
    CircuitTranspilationError: MOVE is not allowed, either because the resonator does not exist,
        the MOVE gate is not available between this qubit-resonator pair, or the resonator state
        is currently in a different qubit register.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker.create_move_instructions
documentation:
Create the MOVE instructions needed to move the given resonator state into the resonator if needed and then
move resonator state to the given qubit.

Args:
    qubit: The qubit
    resonator: The resonator
    apply_move: Whether the moves should be applied to the resonator tracking state.
    alt_qubit_names: Mapping of logical qubit names to physical qubit names.

Yields:
    The one or two MOVE instructions needed.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker.reset_as_move_instructions
documentation:
Creates the MOVE instructions needed to move all resonator states to their original state.

Args:
    resonators: The set of resonators to reset, if None, all resonators will be reset.
    apply_move: Whether the moves should be applied to the resonator tracking state.

Returns:
    The instructions needed to move all qubit states out of the resonators.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker.available_resonators_to_move
documentation:
Generates a dictionary with which resonators a qubit can be moved to, for each qubit.

Args:
    qubits: The qubits to check which MOVE gates are available.

Returns:
    The dict that maps each qubit to a list of resonators.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker.resonators_holding_qubits
documentation:
Returns the resonators that are currently holding one of the given qubit states.

Args:
    qubits: The qubits

Returns:
    The resonators

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker.choose_move_pair
documentation:
Chooses which qubit of the given qubits to move into which resonator, given a sequence of instructions to be
executed later for looking ahead.

Args:
    qubits: The qubits to choose from
    remaining_instructions: The instructions to use for the look-ahead.

Raises:
    CircuitTranspilationError: When no move pair is available, most likely because the circuit was not routed.

Returns:
    A sorted preference list of resonator and qubit chosen to apply the move on.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker._score_choice_heuristic
documentation:
A simple look ahead heuristic for choosing which qubit to move where.

Counts the number of CZ gates until the qubit needs to be moved out.

Args:
    args: resonator, qubit, instructions.

Returns:
    The count/score.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.transpile.ResonatorStateTracker.update_qubits_in_resonator
documentation:
Applies the resonator to qubit map in the state of the resonator state tracker to the given qubits.

Args:
    qubits: The qubits or resonators to apply the state to

Returns:
    The remapped qubits

Class for tracking the location of the $|0\rangle$ state of the resonators on the
quantum computer as they are moved with the MOVE gates because the MOVE gate is not defined
when acting on a $|11\rangle$ state. This is equivalent to tracking
which qubit state has been moved into which resonator.

Args:
: available_moves: A dictionary describing between which qubits a MOVE gate is

  : available, for each resonator, i.e. `available_moves[resonator] = [qubit]`

functions.name: iqm.iqm_client.models._op_is_symmetric
documentation:
Returns True iff the given native operation is symmetric, i.e. the order of the
locus components does not matter.

Args:

: name: name of the operation

Returns:

: True iff the locus order does not matter

Raises:

: KeyError: `name` is unknown

functions.name: iqm.iqm_client.models._op_arity
documentation:
Returns the arity of the given native operation, i.e. the number of locus components it acts on.

Zero means any number of locus components is OK.

Args:

: name: name of the operation

Returns:

: arity of the operation

Raises:

: KeyError: `name` is unknown

functions.name: iqm.iqm_client.models._op_current_name
documentation:
Checks if the operation name has been deprecated and returns the new name if it is;
otherwise, just returns the name as-is.

Args:

: name: name of the operation

Returns:

: current name of the operation

Raises:

: KeyError: `name` is unknown

functions.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.validate_circuit
documentation:
Validates a submitted quantum circuit using Pydantic tooling.

Args:
    circuit: a circuit that needs validation

Raises:
    pydantic.error_wrappers.ValidationError: validation failed

functions.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.serialize_qubit_mapping
documentation:
Serializes a qubit mapping dict into the corresponding IQM data transfer format.

Args:
    qubit_mapping: mapping from logical to physical qubit names

Returns:
    data transfer object representing the mapping

variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Locus
documentation: Names of the QPU components (typically qubits) a quantum operation instance is acting on, e.g. `("QB1", "QB2")`.

variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.CircuitBatch
documentation: Type that represents a list of quantum circuits to be executed together in a single batch.

variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.QubitMapping
documentation:
Type that represents a qubit mapping for a circuit, i.e. a list of single qubit mappings
for all qubits in the circuit.

variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.PRXSequence
documentation:
A sequence of PRX gates. A generic PRX gate is defined by rotation angle and phase angle, Theta and Phi,
respectively.

variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.STANDARD_DD_STRATEGY
documentation:
The default DD strategy uses the following gate sequences:

* Simple symmetric CPMG sequence for short idling times.
* Asymmetric (left-aligned) universal XY4 sequence for medium idling times.
* Asymmetric (left-aligned) universal EDD sequence for longer idling times.

variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.CircuitMeasurementResults
documentation:
Measurement results from a single circuit. For each measurement operation in the circuit,
maps the measurement key to the corresponding results. The outer list elements correspond to shots,
and the inner list elements to the qubits measured in the measurement operation.

variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.CircuitMeasurementResultsBatch
documentation: Type that represents measurement results for a batch of circuits.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.NativeOperation

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.NativeOperation.name
documentation: Name of the operation.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.NativeOperation.arity
documentation:
Number of locus components (usually qubits) the operation acts on.
Zero means the operation can be applied on any number of locus components.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.NativeOperation.args_required
documentation: Maps names of required operation parameters to their allowed types.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.NativeOperation.args_not_required
documentation: Maps names of optional operation parameters to their allowed types.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.NativeOperation.symmetric
documentation:
True iff the effect of operation is symmetric in the locus components it acts on.
Only meaningful if {attr}`arity` != 1.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.NativeOperation.renamed_to
documentation:
If nonempty, indicates that this operation name is deprecated, and IQM client will
auto-rename it to the new name.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.NativeOperation.factorizable
documentation:
Iff True, any multi-component instance of this operation can be broken down to
single-component instances, and calibration data is specific to single-component loci.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.NativeOperation.no_calibration_needed
documentation:
Iff true, the operation is always allowed on all QPU loci regardless of calibration state.
Typically a metaoperation like barrier.
Describes a native operation on the quantum computer.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Instruction

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Instruction.name_validator
documentation: Check if the name of instruction is set to one of the supported quantum operations.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Instruction.implementation_validator
documentation: Check if the implementation of the instruction is set to a non-empty string.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Instruction.qubits_validator
documentation: Check if the instruction has the correct number of qubits for its operation.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Instruction.args_validator
documentation: Check argument names and types for a given instruction

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Instruction.name
documentation: name of the quantum operation

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Instruction.implementation
documentation: name of the implementation, for experimental use only

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Instruction.qubits
documentation: names of the locus components (typically qubits) the operation acts on

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Instruction.args
documentation: arguments for the operation

Native quantum operation instance with particular arguments and locus.

This class represents a native quantum operation
acting on {attr}`qubits`, with the arguments {attr}`args`.
The operation is determined by {attr}`name`.

We currently support the following native operations:

| name    | # of qubits | args                                                                           | description                                                                                                                                |
| ------- | ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| measure | >= 1        | `key: str`, `feedback_key: str`                                                | Measurement in the Z basis.                                                                                                                |
| prx     | 1           | `angle_t: float`, `phase_t: float`                                             | Phased x-rotation gate.                                                                                                                    |
| cc_prx  | 1           | `angle_t: float`, `phase_t: float`, `feedback_qubit: str`, `feedback_key: str` | Classically controlled PRX gate.                                                                                                           |
| cz      | 2           |                                                                                | Controlled-Z gate.                                                                                                                         |
| move    | 2           |                                                                                | Moves a qubit state between a qubit and a computational resonator, as long as at least one of the components is in the $\|0\rangle$ state. |
| barrier | >= 1        |                                                                                | Execution barrier.                                                                                                                         |

For each Instruction you may also optionally specify {attr}`~Instruction.implementation`,
which contains the name of an implementation of the operation to use.
Support for multiple implementations is currently experimental and in normal use the
field should be omitted, this selects the default implementation for the operation for that locus.

# Measure

Measurement in the computational (Z) basis. The measurement results are the output of the circuit.
Takes two string arguments: `key`, denoting the measurement key the returned results are labeled with,
and `feedback_key`, which is only needed if the measurement result is used for classical control
within the circuit.
All the measurement keys and feedback keys used in a circuit must be unique (but the two groups of
keys are independent namespaces).
Each qubit may be measured multiple times, i.e. mid-circuit measurements are allowed.

```{code-block} python
:caption: Example

Instruction(name='measure', qubits=('alice', 'bob', 'charlie'), args={'key': 'm1'})
```

# PRX

Phased x-rotation gate, i.e. an x-rotation conjugated by a z-rotation.
Takes two arguments, the rotation angle `angle_t` and the phase angle `phase_t`,
both measured in units of full turns ($2\pi$ radians).
The gate is represented in the standard computational basis by the matrix

$$
\text{PRX}(\theta, \phi) = \exp(-i (X \cos (2 \pi \; \phi) + Y \sin (2 \pi \; \phi)) \: \pi \; \theta) = \text{RZ}(\phi) \: \text{RX}(\theta) \: \text{RZ}^\dagger(\phi),
$$

where $\theta$ = `angle_t`, $\phi$ = `phase_t`,
and $X$ and $Y$ are Pauli matrices.

```{code-block} python
:caption: Example

Instruction(name='prx', qubits=('bob',), args={'angle_t': 0.7, 'phase_t': 0.25})
```

# CC_PRX

Classically controlled PRX gate. Takes four arguments. `angle_t` and `phase_t` are exactly as in PRX.
`feedback_key` is a string that identifies the `measure` instruction whose result controls
the gate (the one that shares the feedback key).
`feedback_qubit` is the name of the physical qubit within the `measure` instruction that produces the feedback.
If the measurement result is 1, the PRX gate is applied. If it is 0, an identity gate of similar time
duration gate is applied instead.
The measurement instruction must precede the classically controlled gate instruction in the quantum circuit.

# CZ

Controlled-Z gate. Represented in the standard computational basis by the matrix

$$
\text{CZ} = \text{diag}(1, 1, 1, -1).
$$

It is symmetric wrt. the qubits it's acting on, and takes no arguments.

```{code-block} python
:caption: Example

Instruction(name='cz', qubits=('alice', 'bob'), args={})
```

# MOVE

The MOVE operation is a unitary population exchange operation between a qubit and a resonator.
Its effect is only defined in the invariant subspace $S = \text{span}\{|00\rangle, |01\rangle, |10\rangle\}$,
where it swaps the populations of the states $|01\rangle$ and $|10\rangle$.
Its effect on the orthogonal subspace is undefined.

MOVE has the following presentation in the subspace $S$:

$$
\text{MOVE}_S = |00\rangle \langle 00| + a |10\rangle \langle 01| + a^{-1} |01\rangle \langle 10|,
$$

where $a$ is an undefined complex phase that is canceled when the MOVE gate is applied a second time.

To ensure that the state of the qubit and resonator has no overlap with $|11\rangle$, it is
recommended that no single qubit gates are applied to the qubit in between a
pair of MOVE operations.

```{code-block} python
:caption: Example

Instruction(name='move', qubits=('alice', 'resonator'), args={})
```

:::{note}
MOVE is only available in quantum computers with the IQM Star architecture.
:::

# Barrier

A barrier instruction affects the physical execution order of the instructions elsewhere in the
circuit that act on qubits spanned by the barrier.
It ensures that any such instructions that succeed the barrier are only executed after
all such instructions that precede the barrier have been completed.
Hence it can be used to guarantee a specific causal order for the other instructions.
It takes no arguments, and has no other effect.

```{code-block} python
:caption: Example

Instruction(name='barrier', qubits=('alice', 'bob'), args={})
```

:::{note}
One-qubit barriers will not have any effect on circuit's compilation and execution. Higher layers
that sit on top of IQM Client can make actual use of one-qubit barriers (e.g. during circuit optimization),
therefore having them is allowed.
:::

Returns:

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.GateImplementationInfo

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.GateImplementationInfo.loci
documentation: loci for which this gate implementation has been calibrated
Information about an implementation of a quantum gate/operation.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.GateInfo

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.GateInfo.loci
documentation:
Returns all loci which are available for at least one of the implementations.

The loci are sorted first based on the first locus component, then the second, etc.
The sorting of individual locus components is first done alphabetically based on their
non-numeric part, and then components with the same non-numeric part are sorted numerically.
An example of loci sorted this way would be:
(
    ('QB1', 'QB2'),
    ('QB2', 'COMPR1'),
    ('QB2', 'QB3'),
    ('QB3', 'COMPR1'),
    ('QB3', 'COMPR2'),
    ('QB3', 'QB1'),
    ('QB10', 'QB2'),
)

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.GateInfo.implementations
documentation: mapping of available implementation names to information about the implementations

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.GateInfo.default_implementation
documentation:
default implementation for the gate, used unless overridden by {attr}`override_default_implementation`
or unless the user requests a specific implementation for a particular gate in the circuit using
{attr}`.Instruction.implementation`

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.GateInfo.override_default_implementation
documentation: mapping of loci to implementation names that override `default_implementation` for those loci
Information about a quantum gate/operation.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DynamicQuantumArchitecture

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DynamicQuantumArchitecture.components
documentation:
Returns all locus components (qubits and computational resonators) sorted.

The components are first sorted alphabetically based on their non-numeric part, and then
components with the same non-numeric part are sorted numerically. An example of components
sorted this way would be: ('COMPR1', 'COMPR2', 'QB1', 'QB2', 'QB3', 'QB10', 'QB11', 'QB20').

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DynamicQuantumArchitecture.calibration_set_id
documentation: id of the calibration set from which this DQA was generated

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DynamicQuantumArchitecture.qubits
documentation: qubits that appear in at least one gate locus in the calibration set

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DynamicQuantumArchitecture.computational_resonators
documentation: computational resonators that appear in at least one gate locus in the calibration set

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DynamicQuantumArchitecture.gates
documentation: mapping of gate names to information about the gates

Dynamic quantum architecture as returned by server.

The dynamic quantum architecture (DQA) describes gates/operations for which calibration data
exists in the calibration set.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.HeraldingMode

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.HeraldingMode.NONE
documentation: Do not do any heralding.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.HeraldingMode.ZEROS
documentation:
Perform a heralding measurement after qubit initialization, only retain shots with an all-zeros result.

Note: in this mode, the number of shots returned after execution will be less or equal to the requested amount
due to the post-selection based on heralding data.

Heralding mode for circuit execution.

Heralding is the practice of generating data about the state of qubits prior to execution of a circuit.
This can be achieved by measuring the qubits immediately before executing each shot for a circuit.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.MoveGateValidationMode

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.MoveGateValidationMode.STRICT
documentation:
Perform standard MOVE gate validation: MOVE gates must only appear in sandwiches, with no gates acting on the
MOVE qubit inside the sandwich.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.MoveGateValidationMode.ALLOW_PRX
documentation: Allow PRX gates on the MOVE qubit inside MOVE sandwiches during validation.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.MoveGateValidationMode.NONE
documentation: Do not perform any MOVE gate validation.
MOVE gate validation mode for circuit compilation. This options is meant for advanced users.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.MoveGateFrameTrackingMode

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.MoveGateFrameTrackingMode.FULL
documentation: Perform complete MOVE gate frame tracking.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.MoveGateFrameTrackingMode.NO_DETUNING_CORRECTION
documentation:
Do not add the phase detuning corrections to the pulse schedule for the MOVE gate. The user is expected to do
these manually.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.MoveGateFrameTrackingMode.NONE
documentation: Do not perform any MOVE gate frame tracking. The user is expected to do these manually.
MOVE gate frame tracking mode for circuit compilation. This option is meant for advanced users.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DDMode

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DDMode.DISABLED
documentation: Do not apply dynamical decoupling.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DDMode.ENABLED
documentation: Apply dynamical decoupling.
Dynamical Decoupling (DD) mode for circuit execution.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DDStrategy

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DDStrategy.merge_contiguous_waits
documentation: Merge contiguous `Wait` instructions into one if they are separated only by `Block` instructions.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DDStrategy.target_qubits
documentation: Qubits on which dynamical decoupling should be applied. If `None`, all qubits are targeted.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DDStrategy.skip_leading_wait
documentation: Skip processing leading `Wait` instructions.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DDStrategy.skip_trailing_wait
documentation: Skip processing trailing `Wait` instructions.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.DDStrategy.gate_sequences
documentation:
Available decoupling gate sequences to chose from in this strategy.

Each sequence is defined by a tuple of `(ratio, gate pattern, align)`:

> - ratio: Minimal duration for the sequence (in PRX gate durations) as integer.
>
> - gate pattern: Gate pattern can be defined in two ways. It can be a string containing "X" and "Y" characters,
>   encoding a PRX gate sequence. For example, "YXYX" corresponds to the
>   XY4 sequence, "XYXYYXYX" to the EDD sequence, etc. If more flexibility is needed, a gate pattern can be
>   defined as a sequence of PRX gate argument tuples (that contain a rotation angle and a phase angle). For
>   example, sequence "YX" could be written as `[(math.pi, math.pi / 2), (math.pi, 0)]`.
>
> - align: Controls the alignment of the sequence within the time window it is inserted in. Supported values:
>
>   - "asap": Corresponds to a ASAP-aligned sequence with no waiting time before the first pulse.
>   - "center": Corresponds to a symmetric sequence.
>   - "alap": Corresponds to a ALAP-aligned sequence.

The Dynamical Decoupling algorithm uses the best fitting gate sequence by first sorting them
by `ratio` in descending order. Then the longest fitting pattern is determined by comparing `ratio`
with the duration of the time window divided by the PRX gate duration.

Describes a particular dynamical decoupling strategy.

The current standard DD stategy can be found in {attr}`.STANDARD_DD_STRATEGY`,
but users can use this class to provide their own dynamical decoupling strategies.

See {cite}`Ezzell_2022` for information on DD sequences.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.CircuitCompilationOptions

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.CircuitCompilationOptions.__post_init__
documentation: Validate the options.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.CircuitCompilationOptions.max_circuit_duration_over_t2
documentation:
Server-side circuit disqualification threshold.
The job is rejected on the server if any circuit in it is estimated to take longer than
the shortest T2 time of any qubit used in the circuit, multiplied by this value.
Setting this value to `0.0` turns off circuit duration checking.
`None` tells the server to use its default value in the check.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.CircuitCompilationOptions.heralding_mode
documentation: Heralding mode to use during the execution.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.CircuitCompilationOptions.move_gate_validation
documentation:
MOVE gate validation mode for circuit compilation. This options is ignored on devices that do not support MOVE
and for circuits that do not contain MOVE gates.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.CircuitCompilationOptions.move_gate_frame_tracking
documentation:
MOVE gate frame tracking mode for circuit compilation. This options is ignored on devices that do not support
MOVE and for circuits that do not contain MOVE gates.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.CircuitCompilationOptions.active_reset_cycles
documentation:
Number of active `reset` operations inserted at the beginning of each circuit for each active qubit.
`None` means active reset is not used but instead reset is done by waiting (relaxation). Integer values smaller
than 1 result in neither active nor reset by wait being used, in which case any reset operations must be explicitly
added in the circuit.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.CircuitCompilationOptions.dd_mode
documentation: Control whether dynamical decoupling should be enabled or disabled during the execution.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.CircuitCompilationOptions.dd_strategy
documentation: A particular dynamical decoupling strategy to be used during the execution.
Various discrete options for quantum circuit compilation to pulse schedule.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunRequest

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunRequest.circuits
documentation: batch of quantum circuit(s) to execute

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunRequest.custom_settings
documentation:
Custom settings to override default IQM hardware settings and calibration data.
Note: This field should be always None in normal use.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunRequest.calibration_set_id
documentation: ID of the calibration set to use, or None to use the latest calibration set

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunRequest.qubit_mapping
documentation: mapping of logical qubit names to physical qubit names, or None if using physical qubit names

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunRequest.shots
documentation: how many times to execute each circuit in the batch, must be greater than zero

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunRequest.max_circuit_duration_over_t2
documentation:
Circuits are disqualified on the server if they are longer than this ratio
of the T2 time of the qubits.
If set to 0.0, no circuits are disqualified. If set to None the server default value is used.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunRequest.heralding_mode
documentation: which heralding mode to use during the execution of circuits in this request.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunRequest.move_validation_mode
documentation: Which method of MOVE gate validation to use for circuit compilation.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunRequest.move_gate_frame_tracking_mode
documentation: Which method of MOVE gate frame tracking to use for circuit compilation.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunRequest.active_reset_cycles
documentation:
Number of active `reset` operations inserted at the beginning of each circuit for each active qubit.
`None` means active reset is not used but instead reset is done by waiting (relaxation). Integer values smaller
than 1 result in neither active nor reset by wait being used, in which case any reset operations must be explicitly
added in the circuit.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunRequest.dd_mode
documentation: Control whether dynamical decoupling should be enabled or disabled during the execution.

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunRequest.dd_strategy
documentation: A particular dynamical decoupling strategy to be used during the execution.

Request for an IQM quantum computer to run a job that executes a batch of quantum circuits.

Note: all circuits in a batch must measure the same qubits otherwise batch execution fails.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Metadata.dd_strategy

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Metadata.calibration_set_id
documentation: ID of the calibration set used

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Metadata.request
documentation: optional copy of the original RunRequest sent to the server

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Metadata.parameters
documentation: job-specific parameters extracted from the original request

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Metadata.circuits_batch
documentation: circuits batch submitted for execution

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Metadata.cocos_version
documentation: CoCoS version used to execute the job

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Metadata.timestamps
documentation: Timestamps of execution progress
Metadata describing a circuit execution job.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.Status
documentation: Status of a job.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunResult

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunResult.from_dict
documentation:
Parses the result from a dict.

Args:
    inp: value to parse, has to map to RunResult

Returns:
    parsed job result

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunResult.status
documentation: current status of the job, in ``{'pending compilation', 'pending execution', 'ready', 'failed', 'aborted'}``

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunResult.measurements
documentation: if the job has finished successfully, the measurement results for the circuit(s)

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunResult.message
documentation: if the job failed, an error message

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunResult.metadata
documentation: metadata about the job

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunResult.warnings
documentation: list of warning messages

Results of the quantum circuit execution job.
If the job succeeded, {attr}`measurements` contains the output of the batch of circuits,
consisting of the results of the measurement operations in each circuit.
It is a list of dictionaries, where each dict maps each measurement key to a 2D array of measurement
results, represented as a nested list.
`RunResult.measurements[circuit_index][key][shot][qubit_index]` is the result of measuring the
`qubit_index`'th qubit in measurement operation `key` in the shot `shot` in the
`circuit_index`'th circuit of the batch.
{attr}`measurements` is present iff the status is `'ready'`.
{attr}`message` carries additional information for the `'failed'` status.
If the status is `'pending compilation'` or `'pending execution'`,
{attr}`measurements` and {attr}`message` are `None`.

The results are non-negative integers representing the computational basis state (for qubits, 0 or 1)
that was the measurement outcome.

______________________________________________________________________

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunStatus

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunStatus.from_dict
documentation:
Parses the result from a dict.

Args:
    inp: value to parse, has to map to RunResult

Returns:
    parsed job status

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunStatus.status
documentation: current status of the job, in ``{'pending compilation', 'pending execution', 'ready', 'failed', 'aborted'}``

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunStatus.message
documentation: if the job failed, an error message

class_variables.name: iqm.iqm_client.models.RunStatus.warnings
documentation: list of warning messages
Status of a circuit execution job.

classes.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient._retry_request_on_error
documentation:
This is a temporary workaround for 502 errors.
The current implementation of the server side can run out of network connections
and silently drop incoming connections making IQM Client to fail with 502 errors.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient.submit_circuits
documentation:
Submits a batch of quantum circuits for execution on a quantum computer.

Args:

: circuits: Circuits to be executed.
  qubit_mapping: Mapping of logical qubit names to physical qubit names.

  > Can be set to `None` if all `circuits` already use physical qubit names.
  > Note that the `qubit_mapping` is used for all `circuits`.

  custom_settings: Custom settings to override default settings and calibration data.

  : Note: This field should always be `None` in normal use.

  calibration_set_id: ID of the calibration set to use, or `None` to use the latest one
  shots: Number of times `circuits` are executed. Must be greater than zero
  options: Various discrete options for compiling quantum circuits to instruction schedules.

Returns:

: ID for the created job. This ID is needed to query the job status and the execution results.
pylint: disable=too-many-locals

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient.create_run_request
documentation:
Creates a run request for executing circuits without sending it to the server.

This is called inside {meth}`submit_circuits` and does not need to be called separately in normal usage.

Can be used to inspect the run request that would be submitted by {meth}`submit_circuits`, without actually
submitting it for execution.

Args:

: circuits: Circuits to be executed.
  qubit_mapping: Mapping of logical qubit names to physical qubit names.

  > Can be set to `None` if all `circuits` already use physical qubit names.
  > Note that the `qubit_mapping` is used for all `circuits`.

  custom_settings: Custom settings to override default settings and calibration data.

  : Note: This field should always be `None` in normal use.

  calibration_set_id: ID of the calibration set to use, or `None` to use the latest one
  shots: Number of times `circuits` are executed. Must be greater than zero.
  options: Various discrete options for compiling quantum circuits to instruction schedules.

Returns:

: RunRequest that would be submitted by equivalent call to {meth}`submit_circuits`.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient.submit_run_request
documentation:
Submits a run request for execution on a quantum computer.

This is called inside {meth}`submit_circuits` and does not need to be called separately in normal usage.

Args:

: run_request: the run request to be submitted for execution.

Returns:

: ID for the created job. This ID is needed to query the job status and the execution results.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient._validate_qubit_mapping
documentation:
Validates the given qubit mapping, if defined.

Args:

: architecture: Quantum architecture to check against.
  circuits: Circuits to be checked.
  qubit_mapping: Mapping of logical qubit names to physical qubit names.

  > Can be set to `None` if all `circuits` already use physical qubit names.
  > Note that the `qubit_mapping` is used for all `circuits`.

Raises:

: CircuitValidationError: There was something wrong with `circuits`.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient._validate_circuit_instructions
documentation:
Validate the given circuits against the given quantum architecture.

Args:

: architecture: quantum architecture to check against
  circuits: circuits to be checked
  qubit_mapping: Mapping of logical qubit names to physical qubit names.

  > Can be set to `None` if all `circuits` already use physical qubit names.
  > Note that the `qubit_mapping` is used for all `circuits`.

  validate_moves: determines how MOVE gate validation works

Raises:

: CircuitValidationError: validation failed

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient._validate_instruction
documentation:
Validate an instruction against the dynamic quantum quantum architecture.

Checks that the instruction uses a valid implementation, and targets a valid locus.

Args:

: architecture: quantum architecture to check against
  instruction: instruction to check
  qubit_mapping: Mapping of logical qubit names to physical qubit names.

  > Can be set to `None` if `instruction` already uses physical qubit names.

Raises:

: CircuitValidationError: validation failed

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient._validate_circuit_moves
documentation:
Raises an error if the MOVE gates in the circuit are not valid in the given architecture.

Args:

: architecture: Quantum architecture to check against.
  circuit: Quantum circuit to validate.
  qubit_mapping: Mapping of logical qubit names to physical qubit names.

  > Can be set to `None` if the `circuit` already uses physical qubit names.

  validate_moves: Option for bypassing full or partial MOVE gate validation.

Raises:

: CircuitValidationError: validation failed

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient._get_run_v1
documentation:
V1 API (Cocos circuits execution and Resonance) has an inefficient `GET /jobs/<id>` endpoint
that returns the full job status, including the result and the original request, in a single call.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient._get_run_v2
documentation: V2 API (Station-based circuits execution) has granular endpoints for job status and result.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient.get_run
documentation:
Query the status and results of a submitted job.

Args:
    job_id: id of the job to query
    timeout_secs: network request timeout

Returns:
    result of the job (can be pending)

Raises:
    CircuitExecutionError: IQM server specific exceptions
    HTTPException: HTTP exceptions

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient.get_run_status
documentation:
Query the status of a submitted job.

Args:
    job_id: id of the job to query
    timeout_secs: network request timeout

Returns:
    status of the job

Raises:
    CircuitExecutionError: IQM server specific exceptions
    HTTPException: HTTP exceptions

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient.wait_for_compilation
documentation:
Poll results until a job is either pending execution, ready, failed, aborted, or timed out.

Args:
    job_id: id of the job to wait for
    timeout_secs: how long to wait for a response before raising an APITimeoutError

Returns:
    job result

Raises:
    APITimeoutError: time exceeded the set timeout

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient.wait_for_results
documentation:
Poll results until a job is either ready, failed, aborted, or timed out.
   Note, that jobs handling on the server side is async and if we try to request the results
   right after submitting the job (which is usually the case)
   we will find the job is still pending at least for the first query.

Args:
    job_id: id of the job to wait for
    timeout_secs: how long to wait for a response before raising an APITimeoutError

Returns:
    job result

Raises:
    APITimeoutError: time exceeded the set timeout

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient.abort_job
documentation:
Abort a job that was submitted for execution.

Args:
    job_id: id of the job to be aborted
    timeout_secs: network request timeout

Raises:
    JobAbortionError: if aborting the job failed

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient.get_quantum_architecture
documentation:
Retrieve quantum architecture from server.
Caches the result and returns the same result on later invocations.

Args:
    timeout_secs: network request timeout

Returns:
    quantum architecture

Raises:
    ArchitectureRetrievalError: IQM server specific exceptions
    ClientAuthenticationError: if no valid authentication is provided
    HTTPException: HTTP exceptions

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient.get_dynamic_quantum_architecture
documentation:
Retrieve dynamic quantum architecture (DQA) for the given calibration set from server.

Caches the result and returns the same result on later invocations, unless `calibration_set_id` is `None`.
If `calibration_set_id` is `None`, always retrieves the result from the server because the "default"
calibration set may have changed.

Args:

: calibration_set_id: ID of the calibration set for which the DQA is returned.

  : If `None`, use current default calibration set on the server.

  timeout_secs: network request timeout

Returns:

: dynamic quantum architecture corresponding to the given calibration set

Raises:

: ArchitectureRetrievalError: IQM server specific exceptions
  ClientAuthenticationError: if no valid authentication is provided
  HTTPException: HTTP exceptions

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient.close_auth_session
documentation:
Terminate session with authentication server if there was one created.

Returns:
    True iff session was successfully closed

Raises:
    ClientAuthenticationError: if logout failed
    ClientAuthenticationError: if asked to close externally managed authentication session

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient._check_not_found_error
documentation: Raises HTTPError with appropriate message if `response.status_code == 404`.

methods.name: iqm.iqm_client.iqm_client.IQMClient._check_versions
documentation:
Checks the client version against compatible client versions reported by server.

Returns:
    A message about client incompatibility with the server if the versions are incompatible or if the
    compatibility could not be confirmed, None if they are compatible.

Provides access to IQM quantum computers.

Args:

: url: Endpoint for accessing the server. Has to start with http or https.
  client_signature: String that IQMClient adds to User-Agent header of requests

  > it sends to the server. The signature is appended to IQMClients own version
  > information and is intended to carry additional version information,
  > for example the version information of the caller.

  token: Long-lived IQM token in plain text format.

  : If `token` is given no other user authentication parameters should be given.

  tokens_file: Path to a tokens file used for authentication.

  : If `tokens_file` is given no other user authentication parameters should be given.

  auth_server_url: Base URL of the authentication server.

  : If `auth_server_url` is given also `username` and `password` must be given.

  username: Username to log in to authentication server.
  password: Password to log in to authentication server.
  api_variant: API variant to use. Default is `APIVariant.COCOS`.

  > Conviguable also by environment variable `IQM_CLIENT_API_VARIANT`.

Alternatively, the user authentication related keyword arguments can also be given in
environment variables {envvar}`IQM_TOKEN`, {envvar}`IQM_TOKENS_FILE`, {envvar}`IQM_AUTH_SERVER`,
{envvar}`IQM_AUTH_USERNAME` and {envvar}`IQM_AUTH_PASSWORD`. All parameters must be given either
as keyword arguments or as environment variables. Same combination restrictions apply
for values given as environment variables as for keyword arguments.
